title: AWS Adds Multi-Region Replication to Amazon Cognito Identity Service
gist: AWS added one-way multi-region replication to Amazon Cognito, letting apps keep authenticating users from a read-only replica region during outages; it is active-passive only, priced as a per-MAU add-on, and limited to next-gen Cognito user pools.

insights.claim: Amazon Cognito now offers one-way multi-region replication that synchronizes user data, credentials, and configuration from a primary to a read-only secondary region, with both regions recognizing access tokens issued by either, removing the need for custom replication and failover code.
insights.speaker: Renato Losio
insights.so_what: Reduces the build-vs-buy case for third-party auth resilience; incrementally strengthens AWS lock-in against identity competitors.
insights.locator: AWS recently introduced Amazon Cognito multi-region replication

insights.claim: The Cognito replication feature is active-passive, not active-active: in the replica region there are no new sign-ups, password resets, or profile updates outside a failover state, TOTP MFA is unsupported on the secondary, failover is DNS-driven requiring customer-owned custom domain and health checks, and lockout counters are not synced.
insights.speaker: Daniele Frasca
insights.so_what: The limitations cap the feature's usefulness for high-compliance MFA-everywhere workloads, leaving room for Auth0 and others in active-active scenarios.
insights.quote: it's active-passive, not active-active (...) No new sign-ups, no password resets, no profile updates unless you're in a failover state. TOTP MFA isn't supported on the secondary.

insights.claim: Cognito replication is an add-on costing $0.0045 per monthly active user per replica region for Essentials and $0.006 per MAU per replica region for Plus, with machine-to-machine auth incurring an additional 30% charge on top of standard token issuance pricing.
insights.speaker: Renato Losio
insights.so_what: Concrete per-MAU pricing lets buyers model resilience cost; the M2M surcharge matters for agentic/service-to-service workloads.
insights.locator: Replication is available as an add-on

insights.claim: Cognito multi-region replication is limited to user pools on Cognito's next-generation infrastructure and requires a multi-region customer-managed AWS KMS key, signaling AWS is gating new resilience features behind its newer platform tier.
insights.speaker: Renato Losio
insights.so_what: Customers on legacy Cognito pools must migrate to access resilience, a quiet forcing function toward AWS's newer stack.
insights.locator: multi-region replication is currently limited to user pools

insights.claim: Auth0 has long offered multi-region support, meaning AWS is closing a competitive gap rather than leading on identity resilience.
insights.speaker: Renato Losio
insights.so_what: Frames the launch as catch-up; competitive moat in managed identity remains contested.
insights.quote: Among competitors, Auth0 has long offered multi-region support.
insights.locator: On Reddit, the reaction is largely positive
